workItem.title: Recover expired running claims after orchestrator restart
workItem.body:

## Context

A controlled orchestrator restart left game-api#80 labelled agent:running with an expired X1 claim. The ready poller did not reconstruct or release it, so the work disappeared from both running and retry queues until an audited manual release.

## Expected outcome

On startup and every reconciliation cycle, Symphony discovers agent:running Issues owned by this runner, reconstructs valid executions and deterministically releases or resumes expired claims without duplicate AgentRuns, Attempts, workspaces or pull requests.

## Scope

- Query recoverable running Issues through GameApiTrackerAdapter
- Reconcile claim expiry, audit ledger, remote branch, PR and local workspace
- Resume exact confirmed head or release to ready idempotently
- Record recovery decision and receipt in the audit ledger
- Add restart regression tests including a missing local workspace

## Out of scope

- Direct GitHub access
- Distributed leases beyond game-api contracts

## Acceptance criteria

- An expired agent:running claim cannot disappear from the scheduler
- A restart with no local workspace reconstructs from GitHub state
- A valid existing run resumes without creating another AgentRun or PR
- Repeated reconciliation is idempotent
- Recovery decisions are audit-confirmed before irreversible actions
- All Symphony gates pass
